spec_match.rationale: Touches letter-spacing, word-spacing, CSS from the chromestatus summary but skips no obvious gaps — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, input); controls include "Display 56px"; "Headline 40px"; "Subhead 22px" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is shown — no error handling, feature detection, or boundary inputs are surfaced.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link found — the self-heal cleanup script cannot recover this folder if the slug ever drifts.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on headline-tuner
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for CSS letter-spacing and word-spacing: percentage values introduces secondary API members but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://www.w3.org/TR/css-text-4/#propdef-letter-spacing

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on headline-tuner
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link to the see-also section
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule #3: every page must include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link so the slug-recovery script can recover this folder if the name ever drifts.

summary: headline-tuner for CSS letter-spacing and word-spacing: percentage values has structural gaps: the rubric flags multiple fails (see open questions). Needs a pass to convert it from a placeholder into a real concept demo.
